conjugations.Notes: Present 1st person. Regular -AR. Common in health, learning, and progress contexts.
Present 3rd person singular. 'Está a melhorar' = getting better (health context).

conjugations.English Translation: I improved a lot this year.
He got better after the treatment.
You used to improve each day.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu melhoraria mais rápido com ajuda.
conjugations.English Translation: I would improve faster with help.
You would improve with more practice.

conjugations.Notes: Subjunctive 2nd person singular. Very natural: 'Espero que melhores!' after illness.
Present 1st person. Regular -AR. Essential for travel, appointments, and formal communication.

conjugations.English Translation: I always used to confirm bookings.
Did you used to confirm the dates?
She used to confirm everything by email.
We used to confirm by phone.
They used to confirm the arrival.
I would confirm if I knew.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Espero que eu confirme a tempo.
conjugations.English Translation: I hope I confirm in time.

conjugations.Notes: Present 1st person. Regular -ER. 'Depender de' = to depend on. 'Depende!' = It depends! — very common standalone response.

conjugations.English Translation: Do you depend on others a lot?

conjugations.Notes: Present 3rd person singular. Often used as standalone: 'Depende!' = Depends!

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eles dependem do apoio do governo.

conjugations.English Translation: I depended on her at that moment.
You depended on them a lot.

conjugations.Example Sentence: O resultado dependeu de muitos fatores.
conjugations.English Translation: The result depended on many factors.
We depended on them for years.
conjugations.Notes: Preterite 1st person plural. Same as present — context clarifies.

conjugations.English Translation: I used to depend on her a lot.
You used to depend on me.
Success used to depend on effort.
We used to depend on public transport.
conjugations.Notes: Imperfect 1st person plural. Note accent on 'dependíamos'.

conjugations.English Translation: They used to depend on exports.
You will depend on the decision.

conjugations.Example Sentence: O futuro dependerá das escolhas de hoje.
conjugations.English Translation: The future will depend on today's choices.
We will always depend on each other.
They will depend on the funding.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu dependeria de ti se precisasse.
conjugations.English Translation: I would depend on you if I needed to.
That would depend on the situation.

conjugations.Example Sentence: O resultado dependeria de muitos fatores.
conjugations.English Translation: The result would depend on many factors.
They would depend on external support.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Espero que não dependa de mim.
conjugations.English Translation: I hope it doesn't depend on me.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Prefiro que não dependas de mim.
conjugations.English Translation: I prefer that you don't depend on me.

conjugations.Example Sentence: É melhor que dependa de si próprio.
conjugations.English Translation: It's better that he depends on himself.

conjugations.Example Sentence: É bom que não dependamos só de um fornecedor.
conjugations.English Translation: It's good that we don't depend on just one supplier.

conjugations.Example Sentence: É importante que não dependam só disso.
conjugations.English Translation: It's important that they don't depend only on that.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu discuto o assunto com calma.
conjugations.Notes: Present 1st person. Regular -IR. Important nuance: 'discutir' means both discuss AND argue in EP — context determines which.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu discuti o assunto com ela.
conjugations.English Translation: I discussed the matter with her.

conjugations.Notes: Preterite 1st person plural. Same as present — context clarifies.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu discutia sempre com o irmão.
conjugations.English Translation: I used to always argue with my brother.
You used to argue a lot at school.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Ela discutia as decisões da empresa.
conjugations.English Translation: She used to discuss the company decisions.
We used to discuss everything openly.
conjugations.Notes: Imperfect 1st person plural. Note accent on 'discutíamos'.

conjugations.English Translation: They used to discuss the plans.
I will discuss the matter tomorrow.
Will you discuss it with them?

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu discutiria o assunto com calma.
conjugations.English Translation: I would discuss the matter calmly.
Would you discuss it with them?
She would discuss it better one on one.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Quero que tu discutas com ele.
conjugations.English Translation: I want you to discuss it with him.

conjugations.Example Sentence: É importante que ele discuta o assunto.
conjugations.English Translation: It's important that he discusses the matter.
I suggest we discuss it together.

conjugations.Example Sentence: É preciso que discutam a proposta.
conjugations.English Translation: They need to discuss the proposal.
I guarantee it will be ready.
conjugations.Notes: Present 1st person. Regular -IR. Common in formal, professional, and persuasive speech.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu garanti a entrega a tempo.

conjugations.Notes: Preterite 1st person plural. Same as present — context clarifies.

conjugations.English Translation: I always used to guarantee quality.
Did you used to guarantee deadlines?
The company used to guarantee the service.

conjugations.Notes: Imperfect 1st person plural. Note accent on 'garantíamos'.

conjugations.English Translation: I will guarantee the best result.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Espero que eu garanta o prazo.
conjugations.English Translation: I hope I guarantee the deadline.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Quero que tu garantas a qualidade.
conjugations.English Translation: I want you to guarantee the quality.

conjugations.Example Sentence: É preciso que ele garanta a entrega.
É importante que garantam os direitos.
conjugations.English Translation: It's important that they guarantee the rights.
I insist on speaking with you.
conjugations.Notes: Present 1st person. Regular -IR. 'Insistir em' = to insist on (doing something).

conjugations.English Translation: They insist on the same point.

conjugations.Notes: Preterite 1st person plural. Same as present — context clarifies.

conjugations.English Translation: I used to insist until I succeeded.
You used to insist a lot.
She used to insist on her version.
We used to insist on quality.
conjugations.Notes: Imperfect 1st person plural. Note accent on 'insistíamos'.

conjugations.English Translation: They used to insist on the same argument.
I will insist until I succeed.
We will insist on the proposal.
They will insist on the deadline.
I would insist if it were important.
They would insist on the same point.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Espero que eu insista o suficiente.

conjugations.English Translation: I don't want you to insist.

conjugations.Notes: Present 1st person. Regular -AR. 'Recusar-se a' = to refuse to (do something). Essential for expressing boundaries politely.

conjugations.English Translation: I always used to refuse those offers.
He used to refuse to give in.
We used to refuse extra work.
They used to refuse all proposals.
I don't want you to refuse.
I suggest we refuse the proposal.

conjugations.Notes: Present 1st person. Regular -AR. 'Comparar com' = to compare with/to.

conjugations.English Translation: I always used to compare prices.
She used to compare the proposals.
We used to compare the results.
They used to compare the models.
I will compare the two options.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu compararia os dois antes de decidir.
conjugations.English Translation: I would compare the two before deciding.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Quero que tu compares os dois.
conjugations.English Translation: I want you to compare the two.

conjugations.Example Sentence: É importante que ele compare as opções.
conjugations.English Translation: It's important that he compares the options.
I suggest we compare the prices.

conjugations.Example Sentence: É preciso que comparem os resultados.
conjugations.English Translation: They need to compare the results.

conjugations.Notes: Present 1st person. Regular in present. Highly relevant for language learners. 'Traduzir para' = translate into.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Ela traduz do inglês para português.
conjugations.English Translation: She translates from English to Portuguese.
conjugations.Notes: Present 3rd person singular. Note: 3rd person drops the -e: 'traduz' not 'traduz'.
Preterite 1st person plural. Same as present — context clarifies.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu traduzia para o meu chefe.
conjugations.English Translation: I used to translate for my boss.
Did you used to translate the texts?
She used to translate legal documents.
We used to translate as a team.
conjugations.Notes: Imperfect 1st person plural. Note accent on 'traduzíamos'.

conjugations.English Translation: They used to translate the reports.
I will translate the document tomorrow.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu traduziria se soubesse a língua.
conjugations.English Translation: I would translate if I knew the language.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Quero que tu traduzas o texto.
conjugations.English Translation: I want you to translate the text.

conjugations.Example Sentence: É preciso que ela traduza o documento.
conjugations.English Translation: She needs to translate the document.
It's important that they translate well.

conjugations.Notes: Present 1st person. Regular -ER. IMPORTANT: 'pretender' in EP means 'to intend/plan to', NOT 'to pretend'. False friend alert!

conjugations.English Translation: We intend to travel this year.
They intend to open a business.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu pretendi fazer isso mas não consegui.
conjugations.English Translation: I intended to do that but didn't manage.
She intended to solve the problem.

conjugations.Notes: Preterite 1st person plural. Same as present — context clarifies.

conjugations.English Translation: I used to intend to study more.
She used to intend to change her life.

conjugations.Notes: Imperfect 1st person plural. Note accent on 'pretendíamos'.

conjugations.English Translation: They intended to open a business.
She will intend to solve the problem.
We will intend to do more.
I would intend to go if I could.
She would intend to change jobs.
We would intend to travel more.
They would intend to resolve everything.
I hope I intend to continue.
I want you to intend to improve.
It's important that he intends to finish.
I hope we intend to grow.
They need to intend to improve.

conjugations.Notes: Present 1st person. Same conjugation pattern as TRADUZIR. Common in formal, business, and news contexts.
Preterite 1st person plural. Same as present — context clarifies.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu reduzia os custos sempre que podia.
conjugations.English Translation: I used to reduce costs whenever I could.
Did you used to reduce expenses?

conjugations.Example Sentence: A empresa reduzia os custos anualmente.
conjugations.English Translation: The company used to reduce costs annually.

conjugations.Notes: Imperfect 1st person plural. Note accent on 'reduzíamos'.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eles reduziam os preços no inverno.
conjugations.English Translation: They used to reduce prices in winter.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Eu reduziria os custos se pudesse.
conjugations.English Translation: I would reduce costs if I could.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Espero que eu reduza os custos.
conjugations.English Translation: I hope I reduce the costs.

conjugations.Example Sentence: Quero que tu reduzas as despesas.
conjugations.English Translation: I want you to reduce the expenses.

conjugations.Example Sentence: É preciso que reduza o orçamento.
conjugations.English Translation: He/she needs to reduce the budget.
I hope we reduce the costs.

conjugations.Example Sentence: É importante que reduzam os preços.
conjugations.English Translation: It's important that they reduce prices.
